response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack/web engineer, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position requires native-level Japanese, deep C-for-UNIX proficiency, Linux kernel source-code modification experience, and embedded LAN-switch security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 networking). The resume shows no Japanese proficiency, only a brief C/C++ stint (~6 months at National Instruments), and no kernel, network-switch, or security-protocol experience. The bulk of the career is JavaScript/TypeScript/DLang web and product development.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded exposure
Low-latency systems experience with async I/O and high-throughput services at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a hard requirement (native level) for this role
Minimal C/UNIX experience and no Linux kernel investigation or modification background
No experience with networking security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF) or LAN-switch products

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or kernel experience
Solid general seniority and systems fundamentals
